title: allionia gracillima subsp. scabridata
intro: Allionia gracillima subsp. scabridata, also known as Allionia scabrida, is a species of flowering plant in the family Nyctaginaceae. It is a succulent shrub native to the southwestern United States, Mexico, and parts of Central America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues. Herbal preparations of Allionia gracillima subsp. scabridata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are believed to offer health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and promoting skin health.
